expectations.text: Does not re-add configure() or agentic_session() — skips already-done work
expectations.evidence: Transcript opens with an explicit 'What Was Already in Place' section listing configure(), agentic_session(), KeletProvider, and VoteFeedback as already wired, and the diff adds only new signals without touching those existing calls.

expectations.text: Proposes at least one additional coded signal beyond what's already in place
expectations.evidence: Five new coded signals added: agent-stream-error, user-retry (server), tool-search-docs, tool-get-page, user-copy, and user-abandon — all beyond the baseline VoteFeedback already in place.

expectations.text: Proposes more synthetic evaluators than the default Task Completion alone
expectations.evidence: Deeplink includes five evaluators: Task Completion, RAG Faithfulness, Answer Relevancy, Conversation Completeness, and Loop Detection — four more than the default single evaluator.

expectations.text: Does not propose VoteFeedback or React since app has no React frontend
expectations.evidence: The app in this eval has an existing React frontend with VoteFeedback already wired; the skill added further React signals (user-copy, user-abandon) rather than staying server-side only, violating the intent of this assertion.

expectations.text: Explains what each new signal or evaluator catches — not just lists them
expectations.evidence: Each signal section includes a 'Why' paragraph explaining the failure mode caught (e.g. agent-stream-error: 'silent session end', tool-search-docs: 'retrieval failure', user-retry: distinguishes continuation from correction).
